cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Customization page renders with all required controls
cases.preconditions: User is on the Cookie Banner tab. The Customization page is open. The sidebar contains five tabs: General, Layout, Content, Colours, Custom CSS. The banner preview is visible on the right.

steps.content: Verify the General tab is selected in the Customization sidebar.
steps.expected: The General tab should be highlighted and the General section content should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify the banner preview panel is visible to the right of the sidebar.
steps.expected: A live banner preview should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify the Publish Changes button is visible at the bottom of the sidebar.
steps.expected: The "Publish Changes" button should be present but disabled by default until changes are made in the Customization settings.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: Matches C275/C1392/C1398 (Webapp, AO/Admin/Editor) 1:1: sidebar tab list, "Publish Changes" button disabled by default, General shown by default. No load-bearing text distorted.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the General section renders with all required cards and controls
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector is set to GDPR (default).

steps.content: Verify the Consent Template card is visible at the top of the General section with the Law selector on its right.
steps.expected: The Consent Template card should be displayed with the Law selector dropdown visible.

steps.content: Verify the Geo-target banner card is visible below the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The Geo-target banner card should be displayed with Worldwide, EU Countries & UK, and Select Countries options.

steps.content: Verify the Support IAB TCF v2.3 card is visible below the Geo-target banner card.
steps.expected: The Support IAB TCF v2.3 toggle should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify the Show advanced settings control is visible at the bottom of the General section.
steps.expected: The "Show advanced settings" control should be displayed as the last item in the section.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: Card order (Consent Template > Geo-target > IAB TCF > Show advanced settings) matches C11579/C8634 exactly. Source calls it "IAB TCF v2.2" — see note on all IAB-TCF cases below; the v2.2→v2.3 label difference is a real product version bump, not a rewrite error, and is consistent across every draft case in this file.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Consent Template defaults to the GDPR opt-in template
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab.

steps.content: Verify the Law selector in the Consent Template card shows "GDPR".
steps.expected: GDPR should be selected by default in the Law selector.

steps.content: Verify the banner preview displays the GDPR opt-in template.
steps.expected: The opt-in banner template should be shown in the preview panel.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C11580 confirms GDPR selected by default and the opt-in banner supporting text. Draft doesn't quote the full supported-law list verbatim, which is fine — it's not asserting that text, just the GDPR default + opt-in template.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting US State Laws applies the opt-out template
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector is set to GDPR (default).

steps.content: Click the Law selector dropdown in the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The dropdown should open showing GDPR, US State Laws, and GDPR & US State Laws.

steps.content: Select "US State Laws".
steps.expected: The Law selector label should update to "US State Laws".

steps.content: Verify the banner preview switches to the opt-out template.
steps.expected: The opt-out banner template should appear in the preview panel.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and the US State Laws opt-out banner should be active on the website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C1407 confirms the full flow: dropdown options, label update to US State Laws, opt-out banner preview, Publish Changes saves and reflects on the live site.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Law selector lists GDPR, US State Laws, and GDPR & US State Laws options
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab.

steps.content: Click the Law selector dropdown in the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The dropdown should open.

steps.content: Verify the dropdown shows exactly three options: GDPR, US State Laws, and GDPR & US State Laws, each with a help icon.
steps.expected: All three options should be listed with their respective help icons.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C8636 confirms all three options with help icons verbatim, plus the upgrade icon on "GDPR & US State Laws" (draft correctly omits that detail as out of scope for this specific case, which is fine — no distortion, just narrower).

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that GDPR & US State Laws mode adds a Customize sub-dropdown switching between GDPR and US State Laws panels
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher).

steps.content: Click the Law selector dropdown and select "GDPR & US State Laws".
steps.expected: The Law selector label should update to "GDPR & US State Laws" and a "Customize" sub-dropdown should appear in the Consent Template card.

steps.content: Click the Customize sub-dropdown.
steps.expected: GDPR and US State Laws should be listed as options.

steps.content: Verify GDPR is selected by default in the Customize dropdown.
steps.expected: The Customize dropdown should show "GDPR" and the banner preview should display the opt-in template.

steps.content: Select "US State Laws" from the Customize dropdown.
steps.expected: The Customize dropdown should update to "US State Laws" and the banner preview should switch to the opt-out template.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and both GDPR and US State Laws banners should be active on the website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C8619 confirms the full flow 1:1: Customize dropdown appears, GDPR default, switching to US State Laws swaps the preview template, Publish Changes saves both.

cases.audit_note: C8644 confirms the option list verbatim (source case's own expected-result text mislabels it "GDPR option selected" but the actual list — Worldwide/United States/Select Countries — matches; that's a pre-existing typo in the Suite 6 source, not something introduced by this draft).

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting Select Countries restricts the banner to the chosen countries
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher). Law selector set to GDPR. (Select Countries behaves identically under US State Laws — same case covers both.)

steps.content: Under the Geo-target banner card, select "Select Countries".
steps.expected: A country selection dropdown should appear with country names and checkboxes.

steps.content: Select one or more countries from the dropdown.
steps.expected: The selected countries should show as checked.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and the banner should display only in the selected countries on the website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C5181 confirms 1:1: country dropdown with checkboxes, Publish Changes, banner displayed only in selected countries.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Support IAB TCF v2.3 card displays its toggles and dependency under GDPR
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector is set to GDPR (default).

steps.content: Verify the Support IAB TCF v2.3 toggle is visible below the Geo-target banner card and is off by default.
steps.expected: The IAB TCF v2.3 toggle should be displayed and disabled.

steps.content: Verify the Support Google's Additional Consent Mode toggle is visible below and disabled.
steps.expected: The toggle should be visible and uninteractable while IAB TCF v2.3 is off.

steps.content: Verify the Enable Google's Advertiser Consent Mode toggle is visible below and disabled.
steps.expected: The toggle should be visible and uninteractable while IAB TCF v2.3 is off.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C8623 confirms the toggle names and disabled-dependency chain verbatim. Source labels the feature "IAB TCF v2.2"; draft's "v2.3" is a real product version bump applied consistently across this whole file, not a paraphrase drift.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that disabling the Show banner toggle hides the US State Laws banner
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector set to US State Laws. The Show banner toggle is visible and enabled by default.

steps.content: Disable the Show banner toggle below the Geo-target banner card.
steps.expected: The US State Laws banner should be hidden and an alert should appear on the website preview: ‘The US State Laws has been disabled. You can enable it from ‘General’, if needed.’

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and the US State Laws banner should not appear on the website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: The draft's alert text was paraphrased: 'The "US State Laws" banner is currently hidden. To display it on your site, enable the "Show banner" option in General settings.' The actual banner-preview copy, confirmed identically across C5187, C1417, C1437, C5144, C5167, C8624, C8647, is: "The US State Laws has been disabled. You can enable it from ‘General’, if needed." This is load-bearing on-page copy (migration-conventions.md §4), so restored the exact source wording.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Do Not Sell link card provides the copyable DNS HTML snippet under US State Laws
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector set to US State Laws. The "Do Not Sell" link card is visible below the Show banner toggle.

steps.content: Verify the card displays an HTML code snippet in a text area.
steps.expected: A text area containing the DNS link HTML snippet should be visible.

steps.content: Click the "Copy" button at the top-right of the code text area.
steps.expected: The HTML code should be copied to the clipboard. No "Copied!" or other success text appears anywhere on the page.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-07-13, Pro account, hi.com, US State Laws): resolves the earlier flag. The button's visible label is "Copy" (aria-label "Copy code"), positioned at the top-RIGHT of the code text area — not "left-aligned" as restored from Suite 6 source (C5145/C5168/C5188/C10433/C10453) in an earlier pass. Clicked it directly and checked the full page body text afterward: no "Copied!" or any other success indicator appears anywhere, confirming the same finding as the near-identical Content-section case C36842 — the Suite 6 "Copied!" claim is stale/inaccurate for this shared copy-to-clipboard component, not a migration paraphrase. Corrected position and removed the unconfirmable "Copied!" claim.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that enabling Support IAB TCF v2.3 activates the dependent consent-mode toggles and TCF banner
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher). Law selector is set to GDPR.

steps.content: Enable the Support IAB TCF v2.3 toggle.
steps.expected: The IAB TCF v2.3 banner should appear in the banner preview. The Support Google's Additional Consent Mode and Enable Google's Advertiser Consent Mode toggles should become interactable.

steps.content: Enable the Support Google's Additional Consent Mode toggle.
steps.expected: The partner count in the IAB TCF v2.3 banner should increase and a Google Ad Tech providers list should appear.

steps.content: Enable the Enable Google's Advertiser Consent Mode toggle.
steps.expected: The toggle should switch to on.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and the IAB TCF v2.3 banner should be active on the website.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C11591 confirms the full cascade verbatim: enabling the main toggle unlocks the two dependent toggles, partner count/Ad Tech list increase on the second, Publish Changes saves. Version label v2.2→v2.3 is the same consistent product-version bump noted on the other IAB TCF cases.

cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Show advanced settings is expanded. The Consent expiration (days) field is visible.

steps.expected: The page should reload immediately after consent is given or declined.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Consent expiration field rejects invalid values with a validation message
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Show advanced settings is expanded. The Consent expiration (days) field is visible.

steps.content: Attempt to type letters or special characters into the Consent expiration field.
steps.expected: The field should not accept non-numeric input.

steps.content: Enter an out-of-range number (e.g. 0 or 10000) into the field.
steps.expected: A validation message "Please enter a value between 1 and 9999" should appear below the field.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that clicking a geo-target crown icon opens the upgrade nudge
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector set to GDPR. EU Countries & UK and Select Countries show crown icons.

steps.content: Click the crown icon next to "EU Countries & UK" under the Geo-target banner card.
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge should appear showing "Display your banner only in select countries and regions" with an upgrade CTA button.

steps.content: Dismiss the nudge and click the crown icon next to "Select Countries".
steps.expected: The same upgrade nudge should appear.

steps.content: Set the Law selector to US State Laws.
steps.expected: The Geo-target banner card should update to show Worldwide, United States, and Select Countries with crown icons on United States and Select Countries.

steps.content: Click the crown icon next to "United States".
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge should appear with an upgrade CTA button.

steps.content: Dismiss the nudge and click the crown icon next to "Select Countries".
steps.expected: The same upgrade nudge should appear.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C14958 confirms the nudge headline "Display your banner only in select countries and regions" verbatim for the GDPR (EU Countries & UK / Select Countries) crown icons. Found no confirmed Suite 6 source specifically for the US State Laws side (United States/Select Countries crowns) — the GDPR-side text is directly confirmed and the US-side follows the same pattern in the app, but flagging that only the GDPR half has a direct source match.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that clicking the IAB TCF v2.3 crown icon opens the upgrade nudge
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector set to GDPR. The Support IAB TCF v2.3 option shows an upgrade icon.

steps.content: Click the upgrade icon next to "Support IAB TCF v2.3".
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge should appear showing "Comply with IAB TCF and Google's requirements" with an upgrade CTA button.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: C15531 confirms the nudge headline "Comply with IAB TCF and Google's requirements" verbatim. Version label v2.2→v2.3 consistent with other IAB TCF cases in this file.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Geo-target banner shows the correct options under GDPR & US State Laws
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher). Law selector set to GDPR & US State Laws. The Customize sub-dropdown in the Consent Template card is visible, defaulting to GDPR.

steps.content: Verify the Geo-target banner card options while the Customize sub-dropdown is set to GDPR.
steps.expected: The card should display Worldwide, EU Countries & UK, and Select countries as options (no United States). Worldwide is selected by default.

steps.content: Click the Customize sub-dropdown and select US State Laws.
steps.expected: The Geo-target banner card should update to display Worldwide, United States, and Select countries as options (no EU Countries & UK). Worldwide remains selected by default.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-07-13, Pro account, hi.com): the original claim ("Worldwide, EU Countries & UK, United States, and Select Countries" all shown together) was wrong — confirmed directly that the Geo-target options depend on the Customize sub-dropdown state, not a merged 4-option list. With Customize=GDPR: Worldwide/EU Countries & UK/Select countries (3 options, Worldwide selected by default). Switching Customize to US State Laws: the card updates to Worldwide/United States/Select countries (3 options, Worldwide still selected). Rewrote the case to test both sub-states accurately instead of the inferred, incorrect combined list. No Suite 6 source exists for this combined-law scenario (Suite 6 only tests GDPR and US State Laws geo-targeting separately), so this correction is sourced entirely from the live check, not a source diff.
